title: Modular stormwater retention system
abstract: A modular fluid retention system and method for exemplary uses collecting and temporarily retaining fluids, for example stormwater run-off. One example of the system includes a plurality of modular retaining units which are selectively connected together to form an interior chamber volume for collecting stormwater run-off directed into the chamber volume. A plurality of modular trays are engaged with portions of the respective retention units to prevent relative movement of the retention units and eliminate, or substantially reduce, the need for porous material to be installed in and around the retention units greatly increasing the excavation void space usable for water collection and retention. In an alternate application, only a plurality of modular trays are used as the vertical support and fluid retention volume structure for the fluid retention system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A modular water retention system comprising: a plurality of modular fluid retention units, each retention unit comprising: a bottom portion; a top portion connected to the bottom portion, the top portion having a support surface, the top and bottom portions defining an interior water retention chamber volume, the plurality of retention units forming interstitial volume spaces between adjacent retention units; and a plurality of modular trays, each modular tray engageable with at least one retention unit, the plurality of modular trays operable to substantially prevent backfill material from entering the interstitial volume spaces between the plurality of retention units. 2. The retention system of claim 1 wherein each of the plurality of modular trays engages at least one retention unit support surface and extends above the support surface. 3. The retention system of any one of claims 1 or 2 wherein at least one of the plurality of modular trays engages at least two retention units positioned adjacent to each other. 4. The retention system of claim 3 wherein the retention unit support surface is substantially horizontal. 5. The retention system of claim 4 wherein each modular retention unit further comprises: a horizontal support surface defining a top surface having a plurality of recesses, each of the plurality of recess having a lower support surface; and a plurality of tray legs extending through one of the plurality of recesses and abuttingly engaging one of the plurality of retention unit lower support surfaces thereby preventing relative movement of the engaged retention units with respect to one another. 6. The retention system of claim 2 wherein each of the plurality of modular trays further comprises: peripheral sides, the plurality of modular trays positioned adjacent to one another having one peripheral side in close proximity to an adjacent tray peripheral side. 7. The retention system of claim 6 wherein each of the plurality of trays further comprises: a modular key slot defined by one of the modular tray peripheral side; and a locking key selectively positionable in a respective key slot in adjacent modular trays to selectively connect adjacent modular trays. 8. The retention system of claim 1 wherein each retention unit further comprises at least a first and a second opening in fluid communication with water retention chamber volume defining a first through passage between the first and the second opening. 9. The retention system of claim 8 wherein the at least first and second openings comprise a first, a second, a third and a fourth opening, and wherein the retention unit bottom portion comprises four legs defining a first, a second, a third and a fourth side orthogonally positioned with respect to one another and respectively defining the first, the second, the third and the fourth openings, wherein two of the first, the second, the third and the fourth openings positioned along a first chamber axis defining the first through passageway and the other two of the first, the second, the third and the fourth openings positioned along a second chamber axis defining a second through passageway. 10. The retention system of claim 9 wherein each of the first, the second, the third and the fourth sides comprise an arch defining the respective first, the second, the third and the fourth openings, each retention unit further comprising: 

 a connector adapted to selectively connect the plurality of modular retention units to extend the first through passage between connected retention units and increase the size of the interior chamber volume. 11. The retention system of claim 1 wherein each modular tray further comprises: a top surface having a plurality of upwardly angled panels extending toward an apex; and a plurality of sidewalls connected to the angled panels. 12. The retention system of claim 11 wherein the plurality of sidewalls comprise four sidewalls, the modular tray further comprising: opposing corner legs each respectively connected to two of the four sidewalls, each corner leg selectively engagable with one of the plurality of retention units. 13. The retention system of claim 12 wherein at least two of the opposing corner legs engage two different of the plurality of retention units positioned adjacent one another, the modular tray further operable to prevent relative movement between the adjacently positioned different of the plurality of retention units. 14. A modular fluid retention device for use in a void space volume, the device comprising: a modular tray positionable in a void space volume, the modular tray comprising: a sidewall having a peripheral edge; and a top surface having an angled panel connected to the sidewall and extending angularly upward to an apex, the sidewall and top surface defining an internal cavity volume for temporary retention of a fluid positioned in the void space volume. 15. The modular fluid retention device of claim 14 wherein the sidewall further comprises four sidewalls adjacently connected to one another; and the top surface angled panel comprising four angled panels, each angled panel connected to a respective sidewall. 16. The modular fluid retention device of claim 15 wherein the modular tray comprises a plurality of modular trays substantially covering a footprint area of the void space volume, wherein the modular tray sides each further comprise: two corner legs positioned distant from one another; a central leg positioned between the corner legs, each side further defining an open slot positioned between the central leg and each corner leg; two corner seats positioned distant from one another along the peripheral edge and in vertical alignment with a respective corner leg; and a central seat positioned along the peripheral edge and in vertical alignment with the central leg, wherein each of the plurality of trays is operable to selectively form a vertical stack of modular trays through engagement of the respective corner legs and corner seats and the central legs and the central seats of vertically adjacent modular trays, the respective internal cavity volumes of each modular tray in communication with each other. 17. A method of constructing a water retention system, the method comprising the steps of: positioning a plurality of modular fluid retention units having a support surface and defining an interior water retention chamber volume in a cavity having a void space volume, the plurality of retention units defining interstitial volume spaces between adjacently positioned retention units; and positioning a plurality of modular trays to selectively engage the adjacently positioned retention units thereby substantially preventing backfill material from entering the interstitial volume spaces. 18. The method of claim 17 further comprising the step of: connecting the adjacently positioned retention units placing the respective retention unit interior water retention chamber volumes in fluid communication. 19. The method of claim 17 wherein the step of selectively engaging the modular trays to the adjacently positioned retention units further comprises: 

 abuttingly engaging the plurality of modular trays to the respective retention unit support surfaces. 20. A method of constructing a modular fluid retention and management system, the method comprising the steps of: positioning a plurality of modular tray units in a first layer to substantially cover a footprint of a fluid retention cavity defining a void space, each modular tray having a side and an upwardly angled top surface defining an internal cavity volume; positioning at least a second layer of the plurality of modular trays in vertical alignment above the modular tray first layer; and engaging a second layer of modular trays to the modular tray first layer to respectively form vertical stacks of the modular trays in the cavity void space forming a vertical support structure, the modular tray respective internal cavity volumes in communication with the other respective modular tray cavity volumes and the void space.
